report_name: Anodot Breach Leads to Supply Chain Attack on Snowflake Customers; ShinyHunters Claims Responsibility

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A data extortion group that claimed responsibility for breaching Anodot, stealing authentication tokens, and using them to access and exfiltrate data from the Snowflake instances of Anodot's customers for financial extortion.
threat_actors.goals: To steal sensitive data from organizations and extort them for financial payment to prevent the data from being leaked.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters compromised the third-party SaaS vendor Anodot to gain access to its customers' cloud environments, representing a classic supply chain attack.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Application Access Token
attack_patterns.description: The core of the attack involved ShinyHunters stealing authentication tokens from Anodot's systems. These tokens were used by Anodot's service to programmatically access its customers' Snowflake data warehouses.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used the stolen authentication tokens from Anodot to gain legitimate, authenticated access to the Snowflake cloud accounts of downstream victims.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Once authenticated into the victims' Snowflake environments using the stolen tokens, ShinyHunters exfiltrated valuable data from the cloud data warehouses.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: The ultimate goal of the campaign was financial theft through extorting victims by threatening to leak the stolen data if a ransom was not paid.

identities.name: Anodot
identities.description: An Israeli AI-powered cloud cost monitoring and analytics company whose systems were breached by ShinyHunters. Stolen authentication tokens from Anodot were used to launch a supply chain attack against its customers.

identities.name: Snowflake
identities.description: A cloud data platform whose customers were the downstream victims of the supply chain attack. Attacker access to customer Snowflake instances was gained via stolen third-party tokens, not a breach of Snowflake's core platform.

identities.name: Rockstar Games
identities.description: A high-profile video game developer and a customer of Anodot, who was a victim of the supply chain attack. The company was extorted by ShinyHunters after data was stolen from its Snowflake instance.

identities.name: Payoneer
identities.description: An alleged victim of the supply chain attack originating from the Anodot breach.

identities.name: Amtrak
identities.description: An alleged victim of the supply chain attack originating from the Anodot breach.

identities.name: McGraw Hill
identities.description: An alleged victim of the supply chain attack originating from the Anodot breach.

identities.name: Hallmark Cards
identities.description: An alleged victim of the supply chain attack originating from the Anodot breach.

identities.name: Salesforce
identities.description: A platform that attackers reportedly attempted to pivot to after gaining initial access to victim environments.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the ShinyHunters group to post claims of their attacks, name victims, and issue ransom demands for the stolen data.

indicators.name: Anomalous Cloud Service Account Behavior
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Cloud Service Account Behavior
id: 2f8e1c9d-5b7a-4d3c-8e1f-9a0b3c2d1e0f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects anomalous behavior from third-party service accounts, such as logins from new or unexpected IP ranges or attempts to access unusual resources. This is indicative of a compromised service token, as seen in the Anodot/Snowflake supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://rhisac.org/cyber-security-articles/active-data-theft-campaign-targeting-snowflake-customers-via-anodot-third-party-saas-integration-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1078.004
  - attack.t1528
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    UserPrincipalName|contains: '_SERVICE_'
    Status: 'success'
  filter:
    IPAddress|cidr:
      - 192.168.0.0/16 # Replace with known good IP ranges for the service
      - 10.0.0.0/8
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate service account activity from a new IP address used by the vendor.
indicators.description: Detects anomalous behavior from third-party service accounts, such as logins from new IP ranges, which could indicate a compromised token as seen in the Anodot breach.

indicators.name: Unusual Data Egress from Cloud Data Warehouse
indicators.pattern:
title: Unusual Data Egress from Cloud Data Warehouse
id: 8c1d9b3a-7e5f-4c2d-8a1b-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects when a service account performs an unusually large data query or data transfer from a cloud data warehouse like Snowflake. This could indicate data exfiltration using a compromised third-party token.
references:
  - https://rhisac.org/cyber-security-articles/active-data-theft-campaign-targeting-snowflake-customers-via-anodot-third-party-saas-integration-breach/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1530
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: database
  product: generic
detection:
  selection:
    user|contains: '_SERVICE_'
    bytes_scanned >: 1000000000 # Threshold for large query in bytes, adjust for baseline
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large-scale data analysis jobs run by the third-party service.
indicators.description: Detects anomalous data access from a service account, such as an unusually high volume of data being exfiltrated from a cloud data platform like Snowflake.

relationships.source_name: Unusual Data Egress from Cloud Data Warehouse
